question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open a large number of concurrent gRPC subscriptions so that when a network partition isolates the database, each subscription's RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) retry loop continues hammering the scheduler, exhausting the boundedElastic() thread pool and causing a full service denial for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition scenario where topicMessageRepository.findByFilter(newFilter) throws a transient DataAccessException, causing the Flux.fromStream() to emit an error that terminates the entire subscription without any retry or reconnect logic, permanently silencing the subscriber with no notification?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition the Flux.fromStream() call in poll() leaks the underlying JDBC Stream (never closed) because the reactive pipeline errors before the stream's close() is invoked, leading to connection pool exhaustion that persists even after the partition heals?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with limit=0 (no limit) so that during a network partition the RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) loop retries indefinitely at the configured interval, consuming a boundedElastic() thread slot permanently and starving legitimate subscribers from obtaining scheduler threads?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a TopicMessageFilter with a very early startTime so that after a network partition heals, the PollingContext.last remains null (never updated during partition), causing poll() to re-query from the original startTime and deliver duplicate messages to the subscriber, violating message-delivery-once integrity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes poll() to return zero messages repeatedly, leaving PollingContext.last stuck at its pre-partition value, so that when the partition heals the startTime calculation (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1) skips over messages written to the DB during the partition window, causing permanent message loss for that subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open thousands of subscriptions before a network partition, so that the jitter(0.1) on RepeatSpec causes all retry attempts to desynchronize and spread across the interval window, creating a sustained thundering-herd of DB queries the moment the partition heals, overwhelming the database and extending the effective outage?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of any circuit-breaker or backoff escalation in the RepeatSpec.withFixedDelay(interval) configuration so that during a prolonged network partition, every active subscription polls at the fixed interval rate regardless of consecutive failures, amplifying DB reconnection storms proportional to the number of active subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with a very small limit (e.g., limit=1) so that after a network partition the count.get() value is stale, causing the expression (filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) to compute a pageSize of 0, which is then passed to Math.min(0, maxPageSize) = 0, resulting in a zero-row query that silently terminates the subscription without delivering the remaining messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a split-brain network partition where the gRPC service can reach a read replica but not the primary, causing topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() to return stale data from the replica, so the subscriber receives out-of-order or already-superseded messages while the PollingContext.last advances incorrectly, permanently skipping messages once the primary becomes reachable again?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a scenario where a network partition causes the Scheduler (boundedElastic()) to queue an unbounded number of deferred poll() tasks, since RepeatSpec.times(Long.MAX_VALUE) never stops scheduling, eventually causing an OutOfMemoryError in the scheduler's internal task queue that crashes the JVM process?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that Flux.fromStream() wraps a lazily-evaluated JDBC Stream, so that during a network partition the stream is opened but the underlying DB cursor hangs indefinitely (no query timeout configured), blocking a boundedElastic() thread forever and reducing available concurrency for all other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription and then induce a network partition at the precise moment delaySubscription(interval, scheduler) fires, causing the first poll() to fail silently and the RepeatSpec to begin retrying from an uninitialized PollingContext.last (null), so that after partition recovery the subscriber receives all historical messages from startTime rather than only new ones?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the integer cast (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) in poll() during a network partition scenario where count has been incremented by a partial page before the partition, causing the remaining limit to be a small positive integer that, when passed as pageSize, causes the query to return fewer results than expected and the subscription to terminate prematurely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a doOnError() handler in the listen() Flux pipeline so that when a network partition causes topicMessageRepository.findByFilter() to throw, the error propagates uncaught to the gRPC subscriber as a generic INTERNAL status, leaking internal database error messages (e.g., connection strings, table names) in the gRPC error detail?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a subscription with a far-future endTime so that during a network partition the polling loop runs indefinitely at Long.MAX_VALUE repetitions, and since there is no endTime enforcement inside poll(), the subscription never self-terminates even after the partition heals and the endTime has passed, causing the subscriber to receive messages beyond the requested window?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared Schedulers.boundedElastic() instance (a single static scheduler shared across all PollingTopicListener instances) so that during a network partition, one subscriber's blocked poll() threads starve all other subscribers' scheduled tasks, causing a cascading denial of service across all active gRPC streams?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes findByFilter() to return a partially-consumed Stream (e.g., connection drops mid-result-set), so that Flux.fromStream() emits some messages and then errors, leaving PollingContext.last pointing to the last successfully emitted message, causing the next poll() after recovery to start from last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 and silently skip messages that were in the partial result set but not emitted?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition between onNext() updating PollingContext.last via AtomicReference.set() and a concurrent poll() reading getLast() during a network partition recovery, where the partition causes two concurrent poll() invocations to both read the same stale last value and issue overlapping queries, delivering duplicate messages to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user subscribe with limit set to Long.MAX_VALUE (maximum long), so that during a network partition the expression (int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getCount().get()) overflows to a negative integer, causing Math.min(negativeValue, maxPageSize) to return a negative pageSize that is passed to the repository query, triggering an unhandled SQL exception that terminates the subscription?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that during a network partition, each failed poll() call still creates a new TopicMessageFilter via filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build(), allocating heap objects at the polling interval rate, causing gradual heap exhaustion (GC pressure / OOM) proportional to the number of active subscribers and the duration of the partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the doOnSubscribe log statement (log.info(..., filter)) during a network partition to cause log flooding, where each reconnecting subscriber logs at INFO level on every retry, overwhelming the logging subsystem and masking legitimate error signals from operators trying to diagnose the partition?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes the JDBC connection pool to be fully occupied by hung poll() queries, so that when the partition heals, new subscription attempts cannot obtain a DB connection and fail immediately, effectively extending the denial-of-service beyond the actual partition duration?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: listen()] Can an unprivileged external user open a subscription with a startTime set to the minimum valid value (0 or epoch) so that after a network partition heals and PollingContext.last is still null, poll() queries from timestamp 0, causing the repository to scan the entire topic_message table and produce a maxPageSize-row result set on every poll interval, creating a sustained high-load DB query that degrades service for all users?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/listener/PollingTopicListener.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of exponential backoff in RepeatSpec.withFixedDelay(interval) so that during a network partition, all active subscriptions retry at the same fixed rate, and when the partition heals, all subscriptions simultaneously issue DB queries (thundering herd), causing the database to be overwhelmed and re-partitioned or crash?
